channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇪🇺 European leaders gather in London to coordinate Ukraine support
item.description: British PM Keir Starmer hosts Zelenskyy, Macron, and Merz for talks on continued backing for Ukraine as the war enters its fifth year with no end in sight.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Nigerian military rescues 360 hostages from Borno mountain hideout
item.description: Security forces freed 360 men, women, and children held by kidnappers in a mountain enclave in Borno state, in a major operation against militant groups.

item.title: 🇬🇷 Greek police arrest Palestinian man on Crete over Hamas-linked terror plot
item.description: A 37-year-old Palestinian was detained on suspicion of planning terrorist attacks and receiving training abroad, linked to four similar arrests in Cyprus.

item.title: ⚔️ US shoots down Iranian drones over Strait of Hormuz as conflict enters 100th day
item.description: American forces intercepted Iranian drones threatening international shipping in the strategic waterway, while Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed three high-ranking Lebanese soldiers.

item.title: 🇮🇱 One Dead, Four Wounded in Suspected Terror Shooting in Central Israel
item.description: A shooting attack in Kochav Ya'ir area left one person dead and at least four wounded; Israeli security forces captured one suspect and are searching for another.

item.title: 🇬🇷🇵🇸 Arrested Palestinian in Crete Confesses Hamas Membership, Training in Indonesia
item.description: Greek counter-terrorism officers obtained a full confession from the 37-year-old Palestinian arrested on Crete, who admitted to Hamas membership and training in Indonesia.

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenia arrests six opposition candidates day before election
item.description: Armenian authorities detained six candidates from a pro-Russian opposition party on Saturday, one day before parliamentary elections seen as a test of the government's pivot away from Moscow toward the West.

item.title: ⛪ Over one million gather in Madrid for Pope Leo XIV Mass
item.description: More than a million people filled Madrid streets and Plaza de Cibeles for Pope Leo XIV's main open-air Mass during his first papal visit to Spain, in what officials called the largest event of his week-long trip.

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenians vote under Russian pressure to prevent Western drift
item.description: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan faces opposition from pro-Russian parties in a parliamentary election that tests Armenia's efforts to forge peace with Azerbaijan and move away from Moscow's orbit after its 2023 military defeat.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇮🇷 Pakistan delivers special letter to Iran's supreme leader
item.description: Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi traveled to Tehran with messages from the prime minister and army chief as diplomatic efforts intensify to end the US-Iran conflict and prevent further escalation in the Gulf.

item.title: 🇬🇷 Greek police arrest Palestinian man suspected of Hamas attack plot
item.description: Police on the Greek island of Crete arrested a 37-year-old Palestinian man on suspicion of planning terrorist attacks and belonging to the militant group Hamas, authorities said.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇰🇵 Xi Jinping Visits North Korea as Kim Projects Strength
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrives in Pyongyang for a rare meeting with Kim Jong Un, as North Korea showcases its nuclear arsenal and deepening ties with Russia amid stalled US engagement.

item.title: 🇦🇲🇪🇺 Armenia Votes in Pivotal Election Testing Westward Pivot
item.description: Armenians cast ballots in parliamentary elections that will determine whether Prime Minister Pashinyan can continue his push toward European integration despite Russian pressure to maintain Moscow ties.

item.title: 🇪🇺💰 European Central Bank Begins Interest Rate Hikes
item.description: The ECB starts raising interest rates, marking a significant shift in European monetary policy as the continent grapples with economic pressures from ongoing geopolitical tensions.

item.title: 🇸🇬🇮🇳 Singapore Blocks Anti-Indian Content, Traces Origin to China
item.description: Singapore authorities invoke the Online Criminal Harms Act to restrict access to posts targeting the Indian community, saying the content likely originated from China-based sources.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Trump Faces Growing Republican Resistance in Congress
item.description: Republican lawmakers are increasingly breaking with Trump on key policy issues including the Iran conflict as midterm election pressures mount, potentially threatening his agenda.

item.title: 🌍 UN: Hormuz Closure Affects 1 Billion Lives
item.description: The UN Conference on Trade and Development reports that 65 of 75 least developed countries depend on imported oil, with the Hormuz disruption threatening nearly one billion people with rising energy costs and difficult trade-offs.

item.title: 🤖 OpenAI Plans ChatGPT Superapp Overhaul Ahead of Potential IPO
item.description: OpenAI is planning its biggest ChatGPT overhaul yet, aiming to turn it into a "superapp" with coding tools and AI agents to boost revenue ahead of a potential stock market listing, the Financial Times reported on Sunday.

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenians Vote in Pivotal Election Under Russian Pressure
item.description: Armenians are voting in parliamentary elections as the government seeks to loosen ties with Moscow and increase cooperation with the West, facing Russian pressure aimed at preventing a drift away from its sphere of influence.

item.title: ✈️ Airbus Closing In on Widebody Order From Scandinavian Airlines
item.description: Airbus is closing in on a widebody order from SAS, including A330neo and A350 aircraft, with 15 to 20 planes under consideration, Bloomberg News reported on Saturday.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Boeing Can Support China for 200-Jet Order, Executive Says
item.description: Boeing can provide aftermarket parts support to China for a 200-plane order announced following Trump's visit to Beijing earlier this year, a top services executive said on Saturday.

item.title: 💰 High Fuel Costs Could Trigger Airline Failures and Consolidation
item.description: Budget carriers have been among the hardest hit by elevated jet fuel prices linked to the Iran war, and the industry chief warned of potential failures and consolidation ahead.

item.title: 🇪🇺 ECB Set to Raise Interest Rates for First Time Since 2022
item.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde is expected to announce a 0.25% rate increase to 2.25% on Thursday, driven by inflation from the Iran conflict. The move will impact 110,000 Irish tracker mortgage holders and raise borrowing costs across the eurozone.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Raises LPG Prices Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: Domestic cooking gas prices increased by Rs 29 per cylinder in Delhi, bringing the cost to Rs 942. This marks the second hike since West Asia tensions disrupted global energy supplies, with state oil companies still reporting losses of Rs 703 per cylinder sold.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷💰 US explores redirecting frozen Iranian assets to Gulf allies for reconstruction
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has directed a team to assess damage costs for Gulf allies from Iranian attacks. The frozen Iranian assets could be used for rebuilding infrastructure damaged during the conflict.
